name: Snackbar
summary: Transient toast notification (role='status') that fades in and auto-dismisses, pausing its timer on hover/focus; control with `open`/`message`/`autoHideDuration` — use it for brief, non-blocking feedback.

keyboard: Does not steal focus; any action button inside stays in the tab order
Hovering or focusing the snackbar pauses the auto-hide countdown and resumes with the remaining time (WCAG 2.2.1)
a11y.notes: Renders as a polite live region (role='status' / aria-live='polite') so screen readers announce the message without moving focus. Both attributes sit before the rest spread, so error toasts can override them (e.g. role='alert', aria-live='assertive'). Controlled dismissals fade out rather than vanishing. Under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` the slide-in/fade transition is dropped — the snackbar appears and disappears instantly while the auto-hide timer (and its hover/focus pause) is unchanged. Multi-message queueing is intentionally not provided yet — one Snackbar shows one message at a time.

bestPractices: Keep messages short and specific. Snackbars confirm actions or provide brief status; avoid long instructions or multi‑step interactions.
Place them consistently, typically bottom center/right depending on platform.
Offer a single, clear action when needed; prefer undo when appropriate.

Respect motion and timing. Use `autoHideDuration` for transient messages (3–6s typical); longer durations should be deliberate. The countdown pauses while the snackbar is hovered or focused and resumes with the remaining time. The slide/fade transition is automatically suppressed for users who request reduced motion.
For urgent errors, override the default live region with `role='alert'`/`aria-live='assertive'` so it is announced immediately.
Don’t rely on queueing — one Snackbar shows one message at a time; manage your own queue/state if several messages can overlap.
Keep focus behavior intact. Snackbars shouldn’t steal focus; any action button must be keyboard reachable.
Style via tokens/presets. Use theme colors for status (e.g., `primary` / `error`) and consolidate variants with `preset`.
